what: How to build / preview the site
title: VietnamWarData pkgdown site — status & resume checklist
text: Toolchain notes: R 4.6.0; pandoc PATH — point Positron’s bundled copy via RSTUDIO_PANDOC (). Installed work: pkgdown, ggmap, plotly, htmlwidgets, hexSticker, + tidyverse pkgs. Data pre-rendering lives data-raw/release-assets/ (git-ignored).
code: cd ~/Dropbox/r-packages/VietnamWarData-site export RSTUDIO_PANDOC="/Applications/Positron.app/Contents/Resources/app/quarto/bin/tools/aarch64" Rscript -e 'pkgdown::build_site(preview = FALSE)'      # full build # or a single article: Rscript -e 'pkgdown::build_article("explore")' open docs/index.html

title: VietnamWarData pkgdown site — status & resume checklist
text: Config: NEWS.md, inst/CITATION (CMPS DOI), _pkgdown.yml, DESCRIPTION bumped 1.0.0. Reference index: 7 groups; enriched roxygen (@format/@source/@references) 14 getters. New exported helpers: get_province_boundaries(), get_satellite_map() (API wrapper — ships imagery). Fixed get_vciia broken cross-ref + corrected get_basfa docs (returns tibble w/ geometry sfc). Documentation menu: 13 NARA guide PDFs (pkgdown/assets/guides/). Vignettes tab (6): province choropleth, satellite maps, BASFA base areas, HES hamlet security (combines HAMLA + HES-70/71 hamlet category), SEAFA unit locations (incl. 1/11 ACR track), incident counts. Explore dropdown: Overview (landing) · Interactive charts (plotly density heatmap + counts) · Casualties explorer (shinylive/WebAssembly DCAS app: filters service branch, state, year; map + time series + ranked states). App source data-raw/casualties-app/; exported pkgdown/assets/casualties-explorer/ (~84 MB committed; .Rbuildignored package). Articles tab: CMPS preprint PDF + ISQ 2026 placeholder. PDF links open new tab (14). Hex logo (man/figures/logo.png); Vignettes index heading override; “Sabotage” label fix; Title Case headers. Google Maps TOS resolved (Option B): imagery bundled; get_satellite_map() fetches via user’s API key; attributed static PNGs kept; *_satellite_map.RData git-ignored + .Rbuildignored. R CMD check passes clean (Status: OK) — fixed sf-Imports NOTE get_basfa() return proper sf object (sf::st_as_sf()); BASFA vignette updated accordingly. Config/Needs/website added DESCRIPTION (CI installs site deps); docs/ git-ignored (CI builds/deploys ).

title: Mapping Enemy Base Areas (BASFA Polygons)
text: get_basfa() returns sf object (POLYGON geometry), works geom_sf() straight away.
code: library(VietnamWarData) library(dplyr) library(sf) library(ggmap) library(ggplot2)  # One-time: activate your Google Maps Platform API key # register_google(key = "YOUR_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY")  basfa <- get_basfa() se_asia <- get_satellite_map("se_asia")

title: Mapping Enemy Base Areas (BASFA Polygons)
text: base area appears per month tracked, reduce one row per base_area_id mapping. Lay polygons basemap geom_sf(inherit.aes = FALSE), add area’s centroid (center_long, center_lat) point.
code: distinct_bases <- basfa |>   distinct(base_area_id, .keep_all = TRUE)  ggmap(se_asia) +   geom_sf(     data = distinct_bases,     fill = "yellow1", color = "yellow1", alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.2,     inherit.aes = FALSE   ) +   geom_point(     data = distinct_bases,     aes(center_long, center_lat),     color = "black", size = 0.15, alpha = 0.8, inherit.aes = FALSE   ) +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)

what: Active base areas, year by year
title: Mapping Enemy Base Areas (BASFA Polygons)
text: current_status flags whether area "Active" given year. keep active areas, count many years active, facet year — fill shows cumulative years active.
code: active_counts <- basfa |>   st_drop_geometry() |>   distinct(base_area_id, year, current_status) |>   group_by(base_area_id) |>   summarize(     first_active_year = if (any(current_status == "Active")) min(year[current_status == "Active"]) else NA,     last_active_year  = if (any(current_status == "Active")) max(year[current_status == "Active"]) else NA,     any_active = any(current_status == "Active"),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   filter(any_active) |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(c(first_active_year, last_active_year), values_to = "year") |>   group_by(base_area_id) |>   tidyr::complete(year = min(year):max(year)) |>   mutate(years_active = cumsum(rep(1, n()))) |>   ungroup() |>   select(base_area_id, year, years_active)  active_sf <- basfa |>   filter(current_status == "Active") |>   distinct(base_area_id, year, .keep_all = TRUE) |>   inner_join(active_counts, by = c("base_area_id", "year"))  ggmap(se_asia) +   geom_sf(data = active_sf, aes(fill = years_active), color = NA,           alpha = 0.85, inherit.aes = FALSE) +   facet_wrap(~ year, nrow = 2) +   scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("red", "orange", "yellow"), na.value = "grey50") +   labs(fill = "Years active", x = NULL, y = NULL)

title: Mapping Enemy Base Areas (BASFA Polygons)
text: polygons carry CRS (coordinates plain longitude/latitude), align directly ggmap basemap. get_basfa() also includes province, vc_military_region, priority fields can map facet . incident (point) mapping satellite imagery, see satellite article.

title: Explore the Data
text: Animated views combined ground-combat database (get_comb_inc_dta()): year--year 2-D density heat map incidents across South Vietnam, enemy incidents action category incidents per month time. Drag year slider press play; hover zoom. Loads instantly. Open interactive charts →

title: Explore the Data
text: live app (built shinylive) U.S. fatal-casualty records (get_dcas()). Filter service branch, state origin, year death, every view re-counts match: choropleth map casualties home state, casualties time, states ranked casualty count. runs small R session inside browser, first load takes seconds. Open casualties explorer →

title: Combining and Mapping Hamlet Security (HES)
code: library(VietnamWarData) library(dplyr) library(stringr) library(lubridate) library(sf) library(ggplot2)  # South Vietnam outline, dissolved from the province boundaries sv_outline <- get_province_boundaries() |> st_union()

what: Combine HAMLA and HES-70/71 on the hamlet category
title: Combining and Mapping Hamlet Security (HES)
text: Keep hamlet-level records file, line shared columns (renaming coordinates category field), join, translate category ordered score: = 1 (full Allied/GVN control) … E = 5, V = 6 (full VC control).
code: hamla <- get_hes_hamla() |>   filter(record_type == "Hamlet Record") |>   transmute(us_hamlet_id, corps_region_code, date = as.Date(date),             lat, lng, hamlet_category = classification_level_indicator_of_hamlet)  hes70 <- get_hes70() |>   filter(rectp_record_type == "Hamlet Record") |>   transmute(us_hamlet_id, corps_region_code, date = as.Date(date),             lat = hamlet_lat, lng = hamlet_lng, hamlet_category = hcat_hamlet_category)  hes <- full_join(     hamla, hes70,     by = c("us_hamlet_id", "corps_region_code", "date", "lat", "lng", "hamlet_category")   ) |>   distinct() |>   mutate(     hamlet_score = case_when(       str_detect(hamlet_category, "Score A") ~ "A",       str_detect(hamlet_category, "Score B") ~ "B",       str_detect(hamlet_category, "Score C") ~ "C",       str_detect(hamlet_category, "Score D") ~ "D",       str_detect(hamlet_category, "Score E") ~ "E",       str_detect(hamlet_category, "VC Controlled") ~ "V",       TRUE ~ NA_character_     ),     hamlet_score_int = as.integer(as.factor(hamlet_score))  # A=1 ... E=5, V=6   )

title: Combining and Mapping Hamlet Security (HES)
text: Average hamlet’s score across whole period plot coordinates, colored blue (Allied control) red (VC control).
code: hes |>   filter(!is.na(hamlet_score_int), !is.na(lat), !is.na(lng)) |>   group_by(us_hamlet_id) |>   summarise(lat = first(lat), lng = first(lng),             security = mean(hamlet_score_int), .groups = "drop") |>   ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = sv_outline, fill = "ghostwhite", color = "grey70") +   geom_point(aes(lng, lat, color = security), size = 0.05, alpha = 0.5) +   scale_colour_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "firebrick",                          midpoint = 3.5, name = "Avg security", breaks = 1:6) +   labs(caption = "1 = full Allied/GVN control, 6 = full VC control",        x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_minimal()

what: Average security by corps over time
title: Combining and Mapping Hamlet Security (HES)
text: Label record corps tactical zone, track monthly average score. steady decline reflects post-Tet pacification effort (lower = secure).
code: hes |>   filter(!is.na(date), !is.na(hamlet_score_int)) |>   mutate(corp_label = case_when(     str_detect(corps_region_code, "1") ~ "I Corps",     str_detect(corps_region_code, "2") ~ "II Corps",     str_detect(corps_region_code, "3") ~ "III Corps",     str_detect(corps_region_code, "4") ~ "IV Corps",     TRUE ~ NA_character_)) |>   filter(!is.na(corp_label)) |>   mutate(month = floor_date(date, "month")) |>   group_by(corp_label, month) |>   summarise(avg = mean(hamlet_score_int), .groups = "drop") |>   ggplot(aes(month, avg)) +   geom_line(color = "darkgray") + geom_point(size = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(vars(corp_label), ncol = 1) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = 1:6, limits = c(1, 6)) +   labs(x = "Month", y = "Average security score", title = NULL) +   theme_bw()

title: Combining and Mapping Hamlet Security (HES)
text: two HES files also carry dozens sub-model scores (mod1*–mod8* HES-70; *_classification_score HAMLA) want finer measure overall category. corps_region_code 1–4 four corps tactical zones.

title: Counting Incidents: Time Series and Category Counts
code: library(VietnamWarData) library(dplyr) library(lubridate) library(ggplot2) library(scales)  incidents <- get_comb_inc_dta()

title: Counting Incidents: Time Series and Category Counts
text: Count initiation_date year aggressor_side. 1968 Tet Offensive stands peak enemy-initiated activity.
code: incidents |>   filter(!is.na(aggressor_side), between(year(initiation_date), 1963, 1973)) |>   mutate(year = year(initiation_date)) |>   count(year, aggressor_side) |>   ggplot(aes(year, n, color = aggressor_side)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1963:1973) +   scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +   scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Number of incidents", color = NULL)

title: Counting Incidents: Time Series and Category Counts
text: lubridate::floor_date() collapses dates first month finer time series, faceted side.
code: incidents |>   filter(!is.na(aggressor_side), between(year(initiation_date), 1963, 1973)) |>   mutate(ym = floor_date(initiation_date, "month")) |>   count(ym, aggressor_side) |>   ggplot(aes(ym, n)) +   geom_col(fill = "grey35") +   facet_wrap(~ aggressor_side, ncol = 1, scales = "free_x") +   scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y") +   scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Incidents / month")

title: Counting Incidents: Time Series and Category Counts
text: simple count() ranks enemy-initiated incidents enemy_action_category. reorder() sorts bars; coord_flip() makes labels readable.
code: incidents |>   filter(aggressor_side == "Enemy", between(year(initiation_date), 1963, 1973)) |>   mutate(     enemy_action_category = coalesce(enemy_action_category, "Not categorized"),     # tidy up a spelling artifact carried over from the source file     enemy_action_category = recode(enemy_action_category, "Sabotoge" = "Sabotage")   ) |>   count(enemy_action_category) |>   mutate(enemy_action_category = reorder(enemy_action_category, n)) |>   ggplot(aes(enemy_action_category, n)) +   geom_col(fill = "grey35", color = "black") +   coord_flip() +   scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +   labs(x = NULL, y = "Number of incidents")

title: Counting Incidents: Time Series and Category Counts
text: Swap aggressor_side data_file_origin see source file contributes time. Count different column (e.g. general_action_category, prov_name) way. Map counts spatially province choropleth satellite articles.

title: Interactive Charts
text: Incident density time Event counts 2-D density heat map ground combat incidents across South Vietnam, 1963–1973, split initiating side. Use slider step years. Enemy incidents action category — press play drag slider see mix harassment, terrorism, mining, attacks shifted year year. Incidents per month — hover monthly totals; click-drag zoom period (e.g. 1968 Tet Offensive).

title: Interactive Charts
text: Want build ? See satellite counts vignettes underlying (static) recipes, grab data get_comb_inc_dta().

title: Getting Started: A Province Choropleth Map
code: # install.packages("remotes") # remotes::install_github("gl-smith/VietnamWarData") library(VietnamWarData) library(dplyr) library(stringr) library(sf) library(ggplot2)

title: Getting Started: A Province Choropleth Map
text: get_province_boundaries() returns sf polygon layer 45 provinces South Vietnam configured 1966, together corps tactical zone. ships package, download — , importantly, prov_name column uses province names data files.
code: provinces <- get_province_boundaries() provinces #> Simple feature collection with 45 features and 2 fields #> Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON ... CRS: WGS 84 #>   prov_name      corp                       geometry #> 1  An Giang   IV Corp  MULTIPOLYGON (((105.0 10.5... #> ...

title: Getting Started: A Province Choropleth Map
text: get_comb_inc_dta() returns master incident-level table joins VNDBA, SITRA, TIRSA, VCIIA — 638,760 rows covering ground combat South Vietnam, 1963–1973. first call downloads caches file; later calls read cache.

title: Getting Started: A Province Choropleth Map
text: Count incidents per province dplyr::count(), left_join() onto boundary layer. objects key prov_name, join exact — 45 provinces match, leftovers either side. (st_drop_geometry() turns counting step plain data-frame operation; geometry comes back join.) prov_name key across province-coded datasets (SEAFA, HES files, PSYOPS), recipe maps .
code: counts <- comb |>   mutate(prov_name = str_squish(prov_name)) |>   count(prov_name, name = "incidents")  map_df <- provinces |>   left_join(counts, by = "prov_name")  # sanity check: every province matched map_df |>   st_drop_geometry() |>   summarise(unmatched = sum(is.na(incidents))) #>   unmatched #> 1         0

title: Getting Started: A Province Choropleth Map
text: result (pre-rendered):
code: ggplot(map_df) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = incidents), color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.15) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(     option = "inferno", direction = -1, trans = "log10",     labels = scales::comma, name = "Incidents\n(log scale)"   ) +   labs(     title = "Ground combat incidents by province, South Vietnam 1963–1973",     subtitle = "Combined incident database (VNDBA, SITRA, TIRSA, VCIIA)"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Getting Started: A Province Choropleth Map
text: Swap get_comb_inc_dta() get_*() function map different source file. Summarize different column — e.g. group_by(prov_name) |> summarise(kia = sum(n_enemy_kia, na.rm = TRUE)) instead count(). Use corp column get_province_boundaries() summarize corps tactical zone instead province. See reference index full list datasets, Smith (2025) data construction substantive analyses.

title: Mapping Incidents Over Satellite Imagery: Points and Heat Maps
code: library(VietnamWarData) library(dplyr) library(ggmap) library(ggplot2)  # One-time: activate your Google Maps Platform API key # register_google(key = "YOUR_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY")  incidents <- get_comb_inc_dta()

what: A point map: incidents near Hue during the Tet Offensive
title: Mapping Incidents Over Satellite Imagery: Points and Heat Maps
text: Fetch satellite basemap centered Hue get_satellite_map("hue"). Lay incident coordinates geom_point() (use inherit.aes = FALSE points don’t inherit basemap’s aesthetics), facet month watch January 1968 Tet Offensive unfold.
code: hue_map <- get_satellite_map("hue") bb <- attr(hue_map, "bb")   # basemap bounding box  hue_pts <- incidents |>   filter(     !is.na(lat), !is.na(lng), !is.na(aggressor_side),     between(lng, bb$ll.lon, bb$ur.lon),     between(lat, bb$ll.lat, bb$ur.lat),     between(initiation_date, as.Date("1968-01-01"), as.Date("1968-04-30"))   ) |>   mutate(month = factor(     format(initiation_date, "%B 1968"),     levels = format(seq(as.Date("1968-01-01"), as.Date("1968-04-01"), "month"), "%B 1968")   ))  ggmap(hue_map) +   geom_point(     data = hue_pts,     aes(lng, lat, color = aggressor_side),     size = 0.6, alpha = 0.8, inherit.aes = FALSE   ) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("Enemy" = "#FFD400", "Friendly" = "#00FFFF")) +   facet_wrap(~ month, nrow = 1) +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, color = "Initiating side")

what: A heat map: incident density across Southeast Asia
title: Mapping Incidents Over Satellite Imagery: Points and Heat Maps
text: aggregate view, drop points use stat_density2d() Southeast Asia basemap. Faceting aggressor_side contrasts enemy- friendly-initiated incidents concentrated.
code: se_asia_map <- get_satellite_map("se_asia")  sea <- incidents |>   filter(     !is.na(lat), !is.na(lng),     aggressor_side %in% c("Friendly", "Enemy"),     between(lng, 100, 112), between(lat, 4.5, 25)   )  ggmap(se_asia_map) +   stat_density2d(     data = sea,     aes(lng, lat, fill = after_stat(nlevel)),     geom = "polygon", alpha = 0.5, inherit.aes = FALSE   ) +   facet_wrap(~ aggressor_side) +   scale_fill_distiller(palette = "YlOrRd", direction = 1) +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, fill = "Relative density")

title: Mapping Incidents Over Satellite Imagery: Points and Heat Maps
text: get_satellite_map() also provides "saigon" basemap; area, call ggmap::get_map() directly bounding box. province-level static map without satellite imagery, see province choropleth article. published, operation-length–weighted versions figures appear Smith (2025).

title: Mapping Allied Unit Locations (SEAFA)
code: library(VietnamWarData) library(dplyr) library(lubridate) library(sf) library(ggplot2) library(ggrepel)  seafa <- get_seafa() |>   mutate(lat = as.numeric(lat), lng = as.numeric(lng),          record_date = as.Date(record_date))  sv_outline <- get_province_boundaries() |> st_union()

what: How many units were tracked each month?
title: Mapping Allied Unit Locations (SEAFA)
code: seafa |>   filter(!is.na(record_date)) |>   mutate(month = floor_date(record_date, "month")) |>   count(month, country) |>   ggplot(aes(month, n)) +   geom_col(color = "black", fill = "gray", alpha = 0.6) +   facet_wrap(vars(country), scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +   scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y") +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Units tracked per month")

title: Mapping Allied Unit Locations (SEAFA)
text: Drop records without coordinates, plot location South Vietnam outline, faceted year.
code: seafa |>   filter(!is.na(record_date), !is.na(lat)) |>   mutate(year = year(record_date)) |>   ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = sv_outline, fill = "ghostwhite", color = "grey75") +   geom_point(aes(lng, lat), color = "black", alpha = 0.1, size = 1/10) +   facet_wrap(~ year, nrow = 2) +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_void()

title: Mapping Allied Unit Locations (SEAFA)
text: unit reported monthly, can follow battalion’s movements. Filter unit (11th Armored Cavalry’s 1/11 ACR), drop months didn’t move, draw arrows successive positions date/station labels.  data-paper version figure draws track Google satellite basemap. , replace geom_sf() outline ggmap::ggmap(get_satellite_map("saigon")) (ggmap::register_google() key) — see satellite article. package ships Google imagery, version uses province outline instead.
code: unit_move <- seafa |>   filter(unit_name == "1/11 ACR", country == "United States", !is.na(lat)) |>   arrange(record_date) |>   filter(lat != lag(lat) & lng != lag(lng)) |>   mutate(next_lng = lead(lng), next_lat = lead(lat))  ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = sv_outline, fill = "ghostwhite", color = "grey70") +   geom_segment(     data = filter(unit_move, !is.na(next_lng)),     aes(x = lng, y = lat, xend = next_lng, yend = next_lat),     linewidth = 0.5, color = "grey40",     arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm"), type = "closed", ends = "last")   ) +   geom_point(data = unit_move, aes(lng, lat), color = "black", size = 1.2) +   geom_label_repel(     data = unit_move,     aes(lng, lat, label = paste(format(record_date, "%d %b %Y"), "\n", station)),     size = 2.4, box.padding = 0.6, fill = alpha("white", 0.7), max.overlaps = 20   ) +   coord_sf(xlim = range(unit_move$lng) + c(-0.4, 0.4),            ylim = range(unit_move$lat) + c(-0.4, 0.4)) +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_minimal()

title: Mapping Allied Unit Locations (SEAFA)
text: get_seafa() also service (Army/Marine), unit_type, station, control_hq_unit_name counting faceting. Use count(unit_name, sort = TRUE) find frequently tracked units.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Gregory L. Smith. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Smith, Gregory L. (2025). Beyond body counts: Vietnam war ground combat data. Conflict Management Peace Science. doi:10.1177/07388942251391081.
code: @Article{,   title = {Beyond body counts: Vietnam war ground combat data},   author = {Gregory L. Smith},   journal = {Conflict Management and Peace Science},   year = {2025},   doi = {10.1177/07388942251391081},   url = {https://doi.org/10.1177/07388942251391081}, }

what: VietnamWarData: Vietnam War Datasets from NARA
title: VietnamWarData: Vietnam War Datasets from NARA
text: R package downloading working cleaned Vietnam War datasets declassified National Archives Records Administration (NARA). datasets cleaning procedures documented : Smith, Gregory L. “Beyond body counts: Vietnam war ground combat data.” Conflict Management Peace Science (2025). DOI: 10.1177/07388942251391081

title: VietnamWarData: Vietnam War Datasets from NARA
text: Vietnam War, Department Defense implemented unprecedented effort collect data quantify nearly every aspect conflict. Led Secretary Defense Robert McNamara, US military devised metrics intended determine whether winning losing optimize efforts battlefield. resulting files document incident-level information North Vietnamese Army (NVA) Viet Cong (VC) attacks across Southeast Asia, Allied ground combat operations, naval gunfire support, bombing sorties, estimates territorial control, summaries psychological operations, among many topics. Despite impressive scale scope collection efforts, electronic records inaccessible researchers antiquated file formats, indecipherable alphanumeric coding systems, missing delimiters, zoned-decimal storage, incomplete geographic coordinates. package provides cleaned, analysis-ready versions 12 source files, along combined incident-level dataset joins four major ground combat sources single standardized table.

title: VietnamWarData: Vietnam War Datasets from NARA
VietnamWarData: Vietnam War Datasets from NARA
text: package provides access 12 cleaned data files. summary provided ; full details available Smith (2025) accompanying Online Appendix.

title: VietnamWarData: Vietnam War Datasets from NARA
text: download functions share interface. Files cached locally first download subsequent calls load disk without re-downloading.
code: library(VietnamWarData)  # Combined incident-level ground combat database comb_inc <- get_comb_inc_dta()  # Individual source files vndba  <- get_vndba() sitra  <- get_sitra() tirsa  <- get_tirsa() vciia  <- get_vciia()  # Naval operations conga  <- get_conga() nasva  <- get_nasva()  # Casualties dcas   <- get_dcas()  # Unit locations and base areas seafa  <- get_seafa() basfa  <- get_basfa()  # Hamlet evaluation hamla  <- get_hes_hamla() hes70  <- get_hes70()  # Psychological operations psyops <- get_psyops() sortie <- get_sortie()

title: VietnamWarData: Vietnam War Datasets from NARA
text: get_comb_inc_dta() function returns master incident-level ground combat database, joins four incident-level source files — VNDBA, SITRA, TIRSA, VCIIA — single standardized table covering ground combat events South Vietnam 1963 1973. combined dataset constructed six steps: (1) column names standardized across source files, (2) actor labels standardized, (3) column identifying source file added, (4) incidents merged, (5) duplicate entries appearing multiple source files removed, (6) unique incident ID created remaining observation. Key variables combined dataset include: Note combined dataset replace individual source files, contain additional variables included master table.

title: VietnamWarData: Vietnam War Datasets from NARA
text: original NARA files presented significant technical challenges used research. included antiquated binary EBCDIC file formats incompatible modern software, missing delimiters data fields, alphanumeric coding systems requiring hundreds pages documentation, zoned-decimal numeric storage, incomplete military grid reference system (MGRS) coordinates lacking zone identifiers needed convert latitude longitude, fixed period set file structures requiring complex merging procedures. cleaning pipeline file involved: (1) converting binary files ASCII, (2) adding delimiters, (3) converting zoned decimals integers, (4) merging control, fixed, period set files, (5) creating informative column names, (6) reformatting dates times, (7) replacing alphanumeric codes intelligible labels, (8) converting MGRS coordinates latitude longitude, (9) removing duplicate entries. Full documentation step provided Smith (2025) Online Appendix.

title: VietnamWarData: Vietnam War Datasets from NARA
text: get_basfa() function returns dataset spatial geometry columns (sfc_POLYGON) representing boundaries North Vietnamese base areas. sf package required work data installed automatically dependency VietnamWarData.

title: VietnamWarData: Vietnam War Datasets from NARA
text: use data published research, please cite accompanying article:
code: Smith, Gregory L. "Beyond body counts: Vietnam war ground combat data." Conflict Management and Peace Science (2025). DOI: 10.1177/07388942251391081

title: VietnamWarData: Vietnam War Datasets from NARA
text: data files originally collected US Military Assistance Command Vietnam (MACV) archived National Archives Records Administration (NARA).

what: Download the combined incident-level dataset — get_comb_inc_dta
title: Download the combined incident-level dataset — get_comb_inc_dta
text: Downloads caches master incident-level dataset joins VNDBA, SITRA, TIRSA, VCIIA source files single standardized table covering ground combat events South Vietnam 1963 1973.

title: Download the combined incident-level dataset — get_comb_inc_dta
Download the combined incident-level dataset — get_comb_inc_dta
text: data frame 638,760 rows 43 variables. Key variables   include: master_event_id Unique incident identifier. data_file_origin Source file (VNDBA, SITRA, TIRSA, VCIIA). aggressor_side Initiating force (Allied enemy). aggressor_sub_group Specific unit group. general_action_category Broad action type. enemy_action_category Enemy action type (e.g., direct attack,     harassment, terrorism, mining). initiation_date Date incident. initiation_time Time incident. n_friendly_kia Number friendly killed action. n_friendly_wia Number friendly wounded action. n_enemy_kia Number enemy killed action. n_enemy_wia Number enemy wounded action. prov_name Province name. corp Corps tactical zone. lat, lng Geographic coordinates.

title: Download the combined incident-level dataset — get_comb_inc_dta
text: U.S. National Archives Records Administration (NARA), College   Park, MD. Derived electronic records collected U.S. Military   Assistance Command, Vietnam (MACV): VNDBA, SITRA, TIRSA, VCIIA   source files. See Smith (2025) Online Appendix full archival   accession details construction procedure.

text: cache Logical. TRUE (default), downloaded file saved locally reused subsequent calls without re-downloading. force Logical. TRUE, re-downloads file even cached copy already exists. Default FALSE.

what: Fetch a Google satellite basemap for a preset area — get_satellite_map
title: Fetch a Google satellite basemap for a preset area — get_satellite_map
text: Convenience wrapper around ggmap::get_map() retrieves Google Maps satellite basemap one preset areas used Smith (2025). returns ggmap raster object suitable first layer plot via ggmap::ggmap().

title: Fetch a Google satellite basemap for a preset area — get_satellite_map
Fetch a Google satellite basemap for a preset area — get_satellite_map
text: area basemap fetch. One "se_asia", "saigon", "hue".

title: Fetch a Google satellite basemap for a preset area — get_satellite_map
text: ggmap/raster object.

title: Fetch a Google satellite basemap for a preset area — get_satellite_map
text: function **downloads imagery Google Maps Static API call time** — ship cache Google imagery package. Google's Maps Platform Terms permit redistributing map imagery, must supply (free) API key first: preset areas reproduce basemaps used package's mapping articles: "se_asia" (mainland Southeast Asia), "saigon", "hue".

title: Fetch a Google satellite basemap for a preset area — get_satellite_map
text: Requires ggmap package registered Google Maps API key.   use basemaps, please cite ggmap: Kahle, D. Wickham, H.   (2013). "ggmap: Spatial Visualization ggplot2." R Journal,   5(1), 144–161. Map imagery \(\copyright\) Google.

VietnamWarData 1.0.1
text: Added software-citation metadata: CITATION.cff (enables GitHub’s “Cite repository” button) .zenodo.json (Zenodo archiving software DOI). Modernized DESCRIPTION use Authors@R ORCID.

title: VietnamWarData 1.0.0
text: Initial public release. 14 cleaned data files National Archives Records Administration (NARA), originally collected US Military Assistance Command Vietnam (MACV): VNDBA, SITRA, TIRSA, VCIIA (ground combat incidents); CONGA, NASVA (naval operations); DCAS (US fatal casualties); SEAFA, BASFA, HES-HAMLA, HES-70/71 (territory control); PSYOPS-, SORTIE-(psychological operations). Combined incident-level ground combat database (get_comb_inc_dta()): 638,760 rows, 43 variables, joining VNDBA, SITRA, TIRSA, VCIIA single standardized table covering South Vietnam, 1963–1973. Files downloaded demand GitHub Releases cached locally; cache utilities vw_cache_path() vw_clear_cache() manage local store. get_basfa() returns sf geometry boundaries 139 enemy base areas; sf package installed dependency.
